author: jtraglia
content: <@884836500037066862> <@586161934425128960> Sorry guys. I was OOO all of last week. Yes, what you've described is accurate. I'm open to accepting same-epoch proposer preferences but I still think we should disallow it. These preferences are only necessary to receive builder bids for more MEV income. If a validator were to be offline until the epoch of their proposal, I think the node should just self-build and propose that. If we allow same-slot preferences, I might cause a bunch of re-orgs if an attacker repeatedly sends preferences just before their proposal slot.

author: jameshe5018
content: interested in nico's opinion, maybe when making the beacon api spec we just allow current epoch to go through without broadcasting setting local caches to replace /eth/v1/validator/prepare_beacon_proposer and next epoch  actually broadcasts it? if we don't want to allow it for current epoch?

author: nflaig
content:
> These preferences are only necessary to receive builder bids for more MEV income
I don't like this argument, we are moving towards making local building unfeasible so the trustless builder path should be as robust as possible

author: nflaig
content: can't we just add a gossip check which states that `proposal_slot` of the proposer preference needs to be before current slot?
